meaning_english: To be appropriate or applicable to a specific subject. Synonyms: Pertain to, Relate to, Belong to.
To scold or reprimand someone very loudly and angrily. Synonyms: Berate, Scold, Upbraid.
To issue a formal warning or a condition to a statement. Synonyms: Admonish, Caution.
To make a physical or emotional situation less intense. Synonyms: Subdue, Quell, Restrain.
To manage to make a living or a resource last with great effort. Synonyms: Scrimp, Subsist.
To praise someone excessively to get their favor. Synonyms: Flatter, Grovel, Bootlick.
To prepare oneself for a difficult task or a fight. Synonyms: Prepare, Brace, Ready.
To mention or remember something from the past. Synonyms: Recall, Revert, Reminisce.
To speak or write about something with great hostility. Synonyms: Fulminate, Rail, Denounce.
To drop or abandon something that is no longer wanted. Synonyms: Discard, Dump, Shed.
To accept someone else's authority or give in to pressure. Synonyms: Submit, Yield, Succumb.
To accept something eagerly and with obvious pleasure. Synonyms: Relish, Devour.
To waste time doing silly or unimportant things. Synonyms: Fool around, Mess about.
To fall asleep unintentionally while sitting. Synonyms: Doze off, Nap.
To admit that you have done something wrong. Synonyms: Confess, Admit.
To get rid of something by giving it to someone else dishonestly. Synonyms: Foist, Deceive.
To be in agreement or square with something. Synonyms: Coincide, Match.
To control or limit someone or something. Synonyms: Restrain, Curb, Control.
To dishonestly take money from an organization over time. Synonyms: Embezzle, Drain.
To help someone through a difficult period (usually with money). Synonyms: Support, Sustain.
To cause something new to start. Synonyms: Herald, Introduce.
To improve the appearance of something. Synonyms: Revamp, Refurbish.
To remove people or things from a group until only the best are left. Synonyms: Filter, Sift.
To cross something out or to delete. Synonyms: Erase, Strike out.
To give up possession of something or reveal a secret. Synonyms: Surrender, Relinquish.
To focus all your attention on one particular thing. Synonyms: Pinpoint, Focus.
To ask many people the same question. Synonyms: Inquire.
To rely on something. Synonyms: Count on, Depend.
To suddenly stop talking. Synonyms: Go silent.
To think about something negative for too long. Synonyms: Harp on.
